vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of severe biological depletion. The registry-expected specimens (P1, P2, P4) are absent or entirely non-visible. Only P3 (Pothos) retains a vestigial structural presence.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss (Yellow pot is empty/soil only)
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss (Black pot is empty/soil only)
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss (Shared pot with P2 is empty)

plant_audit.P1: Empty soil surface; no botanical activity detected.
plant_audit.P2: Empty soil surface; no botanical activity detected.
plant_audit.P3: Single green petiole/leaf structure remains. The white rabbit anchor is present but the surrounding soil is barren.
plant_audit.P4: Empty soil surface; no botanical activity detected.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Appears dark and potentially saturated, but lacks any vegetative cover.
biome_observations.surface_debris: White particulate matter (likely perlite or mineral salts) visible on the surface of P3.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t-1: Total loss of visible foliage across all pots. The environment has transitioned from a potential nursery to a state of dormancy or total failure.
temporal_deltas.t-1_to_current: Static. No recovery or new growth observed. The P3 specimen remains in a state of extreme stasis.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is suffering from a catastrophic failure of the expected plant population. P3 is the only survivor, though it is severely compromised. The lack of light or improper moisture management has likely led to the total senescence of P1, P2, and P4.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of the white rabbit anchor in an otherwise empty pot (P3) suggests a decorative setup that has outlived its biological components.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical audit reveals a near-total collapse of the indoor desk biome. The registry-listed plants (String of Nickels, Mexican Mint, Silver Guest) are entirely absent, indicating systemic loss. Only a single, isolated Pothos structure remains in the P3 pot. The environment is currently devoid of active growth, and the soil appears to be in a state of neglect.
